Title: Genome-wide survey of the dehydrin genes in bread wheat (Triticum aestivum L.) and its relatives: identification, evolution and expression profiling under various abiotic stresses

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: Member of the TaDHN12 tandem gene cluster on wheat chromosome 6; classified as a YSK2-type dehydrin (Group II). One of the two TaDHN genes whose subcellular localization was experimentally verified in this study.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaDHN12-A1 encodes a YSK2-type dehydrin protein and is part of a tandemly duplicated TaDHN12 cluster in bread wheat.
2. RNA-seq–based expression profiling shows that tandem TaDHN genes (including TaDHN12s) are predominantly up-regulated under cold, drought, and high-salinity stresses, whereas non-tandem DHN1/2/3/6 genes remain low-expressed.
3. TaDHN12-A1 protein is localized to the cytoplasm, as confirmed by TaDHN12-A1–GFP fusion transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaf epidermal cells.
4. The strong abiotic-stress inducibility and tandem expansion of TaDHN12 genes suggest that TaDHN12-A1 contributes to wheat adaptation to dehydration-related stresses and is a promising candidate for stress-resistance breeding.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets SRP043554, SRP045409, SRP300360, SRP041017, ERP013829, and ERP107574 from NCBI were used to quantify TaDHN gene expression (TPM, via Kallisto) across tissues and under biotic (Fusarium graminearum, Blumeria graminis, Puccinia striiformis) and abiotic (cold, drought, heat, salt) stresses.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Cytoplasmic dehydrin whose gene is strongly induced (as part of the TaDHN12 tandem cluster) by cold, drought, and high-salinity stresses, indicating an important role in abiotic stress responses in bread wheat.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dehydrin (DHN) family protein; YSK2-type (YnSKn-type dehydrin with one Y-, one S-, and two K-segments).
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm, confirmed by TaDHN12-A1–GFP fusion transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaf epidermal cells and observed by confocal microscopy.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: As a member of the TaDHN12 tandem cluster, TaDHN12-A1 shows RNA-seq evidence of induction under cold, drought, and high-salinity (300 mM NaCl) stress, whereas TaDHN family members TaDHN1/2/3/6 are largely insensitive. Tandem TaDHN4/5/12 genes are mostly up-regulated under abiotic stress, suggesting stress-responsive expression of TaDHN12-A1.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: IWGSC v1.0 bread wheat reference genome
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Abiotic stresses: cold, drought, heat, and salt (100, 200, 300 mM NaCl); biotic stresses: infection with Fusarium graminearum (FHB), Blumeria graminis (powdery mildew), and Puccinia striiformis (stripe rust); developmental tissues: roots, leaves, stems, spikes, seeds. Subcellular localization tested in N. benthamiana leaves.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Abiotic stress response and gene family analysis of dehydrin (DHN) genes in wheat, including tandem expansion, expression profiling, and subcellular localization.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance traits, particularly responses to drought, cold, and high-salinity (salt) stress.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Triticum aestivum cv. Chinese Spring seedlings for gene expression analyses; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves infiltrated with Agrobacterium GV3101 carrying TaDHN12-A1–GFP in pEGAD-GFP for localization assays.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide in silico identification of DHN genes using HMMER and BLASTP with the dehydrin domain (PF00257), domain confirmation by CDD and InterPro; RNA-seq expression profiling and TPM quantification with Kallisto across tissues and stress treatments; prediction of subcellular localization; cloning of TaDHN12-A1 coding sequence into pEGAD-GFP; Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana; confocal microscopy to observe GFP localization.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TaDHN12-A1–GFP fusion transient expression in N. benthamiana leaf epidermal cells followed by confocal microscopy to validate cytoplasmic localization; RNA-seq–based expression profiling under multiple abiotic and biotic stresses.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as part of a genome-wide DHN gene family survey in wheat using HMMER3.1 and BLASTP with the dehydrin domain HMM (PF00257), followed by removal of redundant hits and domain confirmation with NCBI CDD and InterPro; tandem status inferred from physical clustering and high sequence similarity.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: TaDHN12-A1, as a tandem-duplicated, strongly abiotic-stress-inducible cytoplasmic dehydrin, is a promising candidate locus for enhancing wheat tolerance to drought, cold, and high salinity. Its clear genomic position and associated gene-specific SSRs enable potential use in marker-assisted selection and in transgenic or genome-editing strategies to fine-tune stress responses in bread wheat.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TaDHN7-A is one of three homoeologous TaDHN7 genes that encode SK3-type dehydrins in wheat and represents a conserved SK3 subgroup previously linked to strong abiotic stress tolerance in other species.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaDHN7-A encodes an SK3-type dehydrin protein.
2. TaDHN7-A is constitutively expressed in all tested tissues and generally shows high expression under diverse abiotic and biotic stress conditions, except reduced responsiveness under cold.
3. Subcellular localization of TaDHN7-A–GFP demonstrates that TaDHN7-A protein localizes to the nucleus in N. benthamiana leaf epidermal cells.
4. Together with evidence from SK3-type dehydrins in other plants, the strong and broad stress-responsive expression pattern of TaDHN7-A indicates that it is an important component of wheat’s general abiotic stress response.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets SRP043554, SRP045409, SRP300360, SRP041017, ERP013829, and ERP107574 were used to quantify TaDHN7-A transcript levels across tissues and under cold, drought, heat, salt, and pathogen infections.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Nuclear SK3-type dehydrin with constitutive and generally high expression across tissues and under multiple abiotic stresses, suggesting a central role in broad-spectrum stress adaptation in wheat.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dehydrin (DHN) family protein; SK3-type (three K-segments plus S-segment; SKn-type dehydrin).
Plant_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus, confirmed by TaDHN7-A–GFP fusion transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaf epidermal cells and visualized by confocal microscopy.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: TaDHN7 genes (including TaDHN7-A) are constitutively expressed in all examined tissues (roots, leaves, stems, spikes, seeds) and show generally high TPM values under cold, drought, heat, and salt stress, with TaDHN7-A being less responsive under cold. They are also expressed under F. graminearum, B. graminis, and P. striiformis infection, reflecting broad stress responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: IWGSC v1.0 bread wheat reference genome
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold, drought, heat, and salt (NaCl) stresses; infections with Fusarium graminearum, Blumeria graminis, and Puccinia striiformis; multiple tissues (roots, leaves, stems, spikes, seeds). Subcellular localization assayed in N. benthamiana leaves.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of SK3-type dehydrin TaDHN7-A in broad abiotic stress responses and its subcellular (nuclear) localization in the context of the wheat DHN gene family.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold, drought, heat, high salinity) and general stress adaptation.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Triticum aestivum cv. Chinese Spring seedlings for expression profiling; Nicotiana benthamiana leaves infiltrated with Agrobacterium GV3101 harboring TaDHN7-A–GFP in pEGAD-GFP for localization.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: In silico identification of TaDHN7-A as an SK3-type DHN; RNA-seq expression analysis across tissues and multiple stress conditions; cloning of TaDHN7-A coding sequence into pEGAD-GFP; Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana; confocal microscopy to determine nuclear localization.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: TaDHN7-A–GFP fusion transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaf epidermal cells and observed by confocal microscopy to validate nuclear localization; RNA-seq–based expression profiling under diverse abiotic and biotic stresses.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified as part of a genome-wide DHN search via HMMER and BLASTP using the dehydrin domain HMM (PF00257) on the wheat genome, followed by domain confirmation (CDD, InterPro) and phylogenetic grouping into the SK3 clade (Group V).
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because TaDHN7-A is a nuclear SK3-type dehydrin with constitutively high expression and strong responsiveness to multiple abiotic stresses, it is a strong candidate gene for engineering or selecting wheat varieties with broad-spectrum stress tolerance. Its clear genomic definition within the DHN7 homoeologous group and linkage with gene-specific SSRs facilitate its potential use in marker-assisted selection.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TaDHN4-D1 is one of the TaDHN4 tandem genes on homoeologous group 5 and belongs to the YSK2-type dehydrin subgroup. It shows root-specific expression and strong responsiveness to drought and salt.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaDHN4-D1 encodes a YSK2-type dehydrin and is part of a tandem TaDHN4 cluster.
2. TaDHN4 genes, including TaDHN4-D1, show root-specific expression and are mainly induced by drought and salt stress in RNA-seq datasets.
3. RT-qPCR shows that TaDHN4-D1 is strongly responsive to hormone treatments: it is significantly down-regulated by ABA, but strongly induced by MeJA (peak at 6 h), while GA and SA have weaker or suppressive effects.
4. These expression characteristics suggest that TaDHN4-D1 participates in drought- and salt-related stress responses in roots and is modulated by ABA and jasmonate signaling.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets SRP043554, SRP045409, SRP300360, SRP041017, ERP013829, and ERP107574 were used to quantify TaDHN4-D1 expression under drought, heat, salt, cold and pathogen infection; RT-qPCR under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA hormone treatments was performed on Chinese Spring seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Root-specific dehydrin that is induced by drought and salt stress and shows strong MeJA inducibility but ABA-mediated repression, indicating a role in hormone-regulated root responses to osmotic and salinity stress.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA signaling (negative regulation of TaDHN4-D1 expression) and jasmonate (MeJA) signaling (strong positive regulation of expression), in the context of abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dehydrin (DHN) family protein; YSK2-type dehydrin (YnSKn-type with one Y-, one S-, and two K-segments).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: TaDHN4 genes exhibit root-specific expression; TaDHN4-D1 is mainly induced by drought and salt stresses in RNA-seq datasets. RT-qPCR shows that TaDHN4-D1 responds strongly to hormone treatments: ABA significantly down-regulates its expression (p < 0.01), MeJA markedly induces expression with a peak at 6 h, whereas GA and SA treatments result in weaker or suppressive effects.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: IWGSC v1.0 bread wheat reference genome
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought and heat (from SRP045409), cold (SRP043554), salt (SRP300360; NaCl 100–300 mM), and pathogen infections (F. graminearum, B. graminis, P. striiformis). Hormone treatments: ABA 100 μM, GA 50 μM, SA 100 μM, MeJA 100 μM applied to 2-week-old Chinese Spring seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Function of TaDHN4-D1 in drought and salt stress responses and its regulation by ABA and jasmonate signaling in wheat roots.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress (drought and salt) tolerance and hormone-responsive gene expression in roots.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: T. aestivum cv. Chinese Spring seedlings grown in controlled conditions and subjected to drought, heat, salt, cold, and hormone treatments for RNA isolation and RT-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RNA-seq analysis for TaDHN4-D1 expression under multiple abiotic and biotic stresses; RT-qPCR using LightCycler 96 with SYBR qPCR Master Mix on hormone-treated Chinese Spring seedlings (ABA, GA, SA, MeJA) to quantify TaDHN4-D1 expression over time (0, 3, 6, 12 h).
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time-course of TaDHN4-D1 under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA treatments, combined with RNA-seq expression profiles under drought and salt stress, to validate its hormone and stress responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified via genome-wide DHN family search using HMMER/BLASTP with PF00257 on the wheat genome, then categorized as a tandem YSK2-type DHN4 gene based on chromosomal clustering and phylogenetic analysis.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Given its root-specific expression, strong induction by drought and salt, and pronounced MeJA responsiveness, TaDHN4-D1 is a promising candidate gene for improving root-based tolerance to water deficit and salinity in wheat. Its tandem location and clear expression signature make it suitable as a target for functional validation and for marker-assisted selection in stress-resilient cultivars.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TaDHN7-B is the B-genome homoeolog of the TaDHN7 SK3-type dehydrin genes and was one of six TaDHNs chosen for detailed hormone response analysis.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaDHN7-B encodes an SK3-type dehydrin.
2. TaDHN7 genes (including TaDHN7-B) are constitutively expressed in all tissues and generally show high expression across diverse abiotic stresses.
3. RT-qPCR shows that TaDHN7-B is strongly induced by ABA and MeJA and significantly up-regulated by SA, whereas GA has only weak effects.
4. The combination of broad stress expression and strong responsiveness to ABA, SA, and MeJA suggests that TaDHN7-B is an important integrator of hormone and stress signaling in wheat.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets SRP043554, SRP045409, SRP300360, SRP041017, ERP013829, and ERP107574 were used to profile TaDHN7-B expression under abiotic and biotic stresses; RT-qPCR examined TaDHN7-B expression under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA treatments.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SK3-type dehydrin with constitutive and high expression under multiple abiotic stresses and strong induction by ABA, SA, and MeJA, indicating a key role in hormone-mediated and general abiotic stress responses in wheat.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA signaling (strong positive regulation of TaDHN7-B expression), salicylic acid signaling (significant up-regulation), and jasmonate (MeJA) signaling (sustained induction throughout treatment), in the context of abiotic and biotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: TaDHN7 genes are constitutively expressed in roots, leaves, stems, spikes, and seeds and are generally highly expressed under cold, drought, heat, and salt stress (with TaDHN7-A less responsive to cold). RT-qPCR shows that TaDHN7-B is strongly induced by ABA and MeJA and significantly up-regulated by SA (p < 0.01), while GA treatment causes only weak induction or inhibition.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: IWGSC v1.0 bread wheat reference genome
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold, drought, heat, and salt stresses; infections with F. graminearum, B. graminis, and P. striiformis; hormone treatments with ABA 100 μM, GA 50 μM, SA 100 μM, MeJA 100 μM applied to Chinese Spring seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Integration of ABA, SA, and MeJA hormone signals with abiotic stress responses via the SK3-type dehydrin TaDHN7-B in wheat.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold, drought, heat, salt) and hormone-responsive stress signaling (ABA, SA, MeJA).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: T. aestivum cv. Chinese Spring seedlings grown under controlled conditions and subjected to hormone and stress treatments for RNA extraction and RT-qPCR.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: RNA-seq–based expression profiling for TaDHN7-B across tissues and stress treatments; RT-qPCR time-course under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA hormone treatments to characterize dynamic expression responses.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time-course of TaDHN7-B under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA treatments combined with RNA-seq profiles under abiotic and biotic stresses to validate its strong hormone and stress responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified via genome-wide dehydrin family mining (HMMER/BLASTP using PF00257), followed by phylogenetic grouping into the SK3-type DHN7 clade and assignment to the B sub-genome based on synteny and chromosomal distribution.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: TaDHN7-B’s broad stress responsiveness and strong induction by ABA, SA, and MeJA make it an attractive candidate for improving multi-stress tolerance and for fine-tuning hormone-regulated defense and adaptation pathways in wheat breeding programs.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TaDHN8-D (annotated as TaDHN8-Da in Table 1) is a Kn-type dehydrin belonging to Group I, a clade whose members are strongly up-regulated by cold and drought but are often down-regulated by certain biotic stresses.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaDHN8-D encodes a Kn-type dehydrin and is part of the Group I Kn-type DHNs (DHN8/9/10/13).
2. Group I DHNs are mainly up-regulated under cold and drought stress and show sensitivity to high salinity, while they are largely down-regulated during B. graminis and P. striiformis infections.
3. RT-qPCR reveals that TaDHN8-D is strongly induced by ABA and MeJA and remains up-regulated throughout the MeJA treatment period, whereas GA and SA exert weaker effects.
4. These features support a role for TaDHN8-D in ABA- and jasmonate-mediated responses to dehydration-related stresses.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets covering cold, drought, heat, salt, and pathogen infection (SRP043554, SRP045409, SRP300360, SRP041017, ERP013829) plus tissue data (ERP107574) were re-analyzed to assess TaDHN8-D expression; RT-qPCR tracked its response to ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Kn-type dehydrin that is induced under cold and drought and is highly responsive to ABA and especially MeJA, remaining up-regulated throughout MeJA treatment, indicative of a role in hormone-modulated abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA signaling (positive regulation of TaDHN8-D expression) and jasmonate (MeJA) signaling (sustained strong induction), within the context of cold, drought, and high-salinity stress responses; reduced expression under certain fungal infections (B. graminis, P. striiformis).
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dehydrin (DHN) family protein; K3-type Kn dehydrin (three K-segments, lacking Y- and S-segments).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: As part of Group I Kn-type DHNs (DHN8/9/10/13), TaDHN8-D is mainly up-regulated under cold and drought stress and shows sensitivity to high salinity (notably at 300 mM NaCl), while it is mostly down-regulated during B. graminis and P. striiformis infections. RT-qPCR shows strong ABA- and MeJA-induced expression, with TaDHN8-D remaining up-regulated throughout the entire MeJA treatment period.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: IWGSC v1.0 bread wheat reference genome
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold, drought, heat, and salt (100–300 mM NaCl) stresses; infections with Fusarium graminearum, Blumeria graminis, and Puccinia striiformis; hormone treatments with ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA in Chinese Spring seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of Kn-type dehydrin TaDHN8-D in ABA- and MeJA-regulated cold, drought, and salt stress responses in wheat.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold, drought, high salinity) and hormone-responsive gene expression (ABA and jasmonate), with altered expression under biotic stress (fungal infection).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: T. aestivum cv. Chinese Spring seedlings subjected to abiotic and biotic stresses and hormone treatments; RNA extracted for RNA-seq and RT-qPCR validation.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification and classification of TaDHN8-D as a Kn-type DHN; RNA-seq expression profiling under multiple stresses; RT-qPCR analysis of TaDHN8-D expression under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA hormone treatments.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time-course of TaDHN8-D expression under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA treatments combined with RNA-seq profiles under cold, drought, salt, and pathogen stresses to validate its hormone and stress responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Discovered via HMMER/BLASTP scanning of the wheat genome for dehydrin domains (PF00257), followed by phylogenetic classification into Group I Kn-type DHNs and assignment as the D-genome TaDHN8 homoeolog.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because TaDHN8-D is a Kn-type dehydrin strongly induced by cold and drought and highly responsive to ABA and MeJA, it represents a useful candidate for breeding or engineering wheat with enhanced tolerance to dehydration-related stresses, particularly in environments where hormone-mediated stress signaling is critical.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TaDHN9-A is a Kn-type dehydrin in Group I and one of the six TaDHN genes subjected to detailed hormone-response analysis; it exhibits particularly strong ABA- and MeJA-induced expression.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaDHN9-A encodes a K2-type Kn dehydrin and is part of the Group I DHN8/9/10/13 cluster.
2. Group I DHNs (including TaDHN9-A) are mainly up-regulated under cold and drought and show sensitivity to high salinity, but are mostly down-regulated during B. graminis and P. striiformis infection.
3. RT-qPCR reveals that TaDHN9-A is strongly induced by ABA and MeJA (peaking at 12 h under MeJA) and significantly up-regulated by SA, with GA causing only weak changes.
4. The combination of strong hormone responsiveness and abiotic stress induction indicates that TaDHN9-A is a key hormone-responsive dehydrin in wheat stress adaptation.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets for wheat under cold, drought, heat, salt, and pathogen infection plus tissue expression (SRP043554, SRP045409, SRP300360, SRP041017, ERP013829, ERP107574) were used to assess TaDHN9-A expression; RT-qPCR characterized its response to ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Strongly hormone-responsive Kn-type dehydrin, with particularly high induction by ABA and MeJA and SA-responsive expression, and up-regulation under cold, drought, and high-salinity stress, pointing to a central role in hormone-modulated abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA signaling (marked positive regulation of TaDHN9-A expression), salicylic acid signaling (significant up-regulation), and jasmonate (MeJA) signaling (strong induction with peak at 12 h), associated with abiotic stress responses to cold, drought, and high salinity.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dehydrin (DHN) family protein; K2-type Kn dehydrin (two K-segments).
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: As part of Group I DHNs, TaDHN9-A is mainly up-regulated under cold and drought and shows sensitivity to high salinity, while being mostly down-regulated during B. graminis and P. striiformis infection. RT-qPCR shows that ABA induces strong expression, SA significantly up-regulates TaDHN9-A (p < 0.01), and MeJA markedly induces expression with a peak at 12 h; GA causes only weak induction or inhibition.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: IWGSC v1.0 bread wheat reference genome
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold, drought, heat, and salt stresses; infections with F. graminearum, B. graminis, and P. striiformis; hormone treatments with ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA (100/50/100/100 μM) in Chinese Spring seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Hormone-regulated expression and abiotic stress responsiveness of Kn-type dehydrin TaDHN9-A in wheat.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold, drought, high salinity) and hormone-responsive regulation (ABA, SA, MeJA) of stress-related gene expression.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Chinese Spring seedlings grown in a controlled growth chamber and subjected to various stresses and hormone treatments; RNA extracted for RNA-seq and RT-qPCR analyses of TaDHN9-A.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification of TaDHN9-A as a Kn-type DHN; RNA-seq profiling under abiotic and biotic stress; RT-qPCR time-course under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA treatments to characterize hormone-dependent expression dynamics.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time-course of TaDHN9-A under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA treatments combined with RNA-seq stress-expression data to validate its strong hormone and abiotic-stress responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified from the wheat genome using HMMER/BLASTP search for PF00257 dehydrin domains, followed by phylogenetic placement in the Group I Kn-type DHNs and positional assignment as the A-genome TaDHN9 homoeolog.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: Because TaDHN9-A is a strongly ABA-, SA-, and MeJA-responsive Kn-type dehydrin that is induced by cold, drought, and high salinity, it is a particularly attractive target for breeding or engineering wheat with enhanced, hormone-integrated tolerance to multiple abiotic stresses.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TaDHN12-B5 is a B-genome member of the TaDHN12 tandem cluster of YSK2-type dehydrins; it is notable for its strong ABA and MeJA inducibility and up-regulation under Fusarium graminearum infection.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaDHN12-B5 encodes a YSK2-type dehydrin and belongs to the TaDHN12 tandem gene cluster.
2. Tandem TaDHN12 genes are mostly up-regulated under cold, drought, and high-salinity stress in RNA-seq datasets.
3. Under F. graminearum infection, TaDHN12-B5 is up-regulated at 72 h, whereas the DHN family as a whole responds weakly to most biotic stresses.
4. RT-qPCR shows that TaDHN12-B5 is strongly induced by ABA and MeJA (with expression peaking at 12 h under MeJA), while GA and SA have relatively minor or negative effects.
5. These characteristics identify TaDHN12-B5 as a hormone-responsive, tandem-duplicated dehydrin implicated in abiotic and specific biotic (Fusarium) stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets for wheat under tissue, abiotic, and biotic stress conditions (SRP043554, SRP045409, SRP300360, SRP041017, ERP013829, ERP107574) were used to quantify TaDHN12-B5 expression; RT-qPCR measured its response to ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA treatments.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tandem YSK2-type dehydrin that is up-regulated under drought, cold, high salinity, and Fusarium graminearum infection and shows strikingly high ABA- and strong MeJA-induced expression, indicating a role at the intersection of hormone signaling and stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA signaling (very strong positive regulation of TaDHN12-B5 expression), jasmonate (MeJA) signaling (induction peaking at 12 h), and possible involvement in pathogen-triggered responses to Fusarium graminearum; GA and SA have weaker or inhibitory effects.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: As a member of the TaDHN12 tandem cluster, TaDHN12-B5 is among the DHN genes up-regulated under cold, drought, and high-salinity stress, while many non-tandem DHNs show low responsiveness. Under F. graminearum infection, TaDHN12-B5 is up-regulated at 72 h. RT-qPCR shows strong and sensitive ABA-induced expression (one of the two genes with strikingly high ABA responsiveness) and MeJA-induced expression peaking at 12 h, with GA and SA causing weaker or negative effects.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: IWGSC v1.0 bread wheat reference genome
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold, drought, heat, and salt (100–300 mM NaCl) stresses; infection with Fusarium graminearum (FHB) and other pathogens; hormone treatments with ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA applied to Chinese Spring seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Role of tandem YSK2-type dehydrin TaDHN12-B5 in ABA/MeJA-regulated responses to abiotic stresses and Fusarium head blight in wheat.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold, drought, high salinity) and biotic stress response (Fusarium head blight), with hormone-responsive regulation by ABA and MeJA.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Chinese Spring seedlings under multiple stress and hormone treatments; RNA prepared for RNA-seq and RT-qPCR analyses.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: Genome-wide identification of TaDHN12-B5 as part of the TaDHN12 tandem cluster; RNA-seq profiling under abiotic and biotic stresses including Fusarium infection; RT-qPCR time-course under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA treatments to evaluate hormone inducibility.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time-course of TaDHN12-B5 under hormone treatments combined with RNA-seq analysis under abiotic stresses and Fusarium infection to validate its stress and hormone responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified via HMMER/BLASTP search for dehydrin-domain proteins in wheat, followed by tandem clustering and phylogenetic analysis indicating membership in the TaDHN12 YSK2-type dehydrin group; assigned to the B sub-genome.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: TaDHN12-B5, with strong ABA and MeJA inducibility and responsiveness to both abiotic stresses and Fusarium infection, is a promising candidate for breeding wheat with combined abiotic and disease stress tolerance and for developing stress- and hormone-responsive markers based on DHN-specific SSRs.

Plant_Genes.Notes_or_Other_Important_Info: TaDHN13-A is a Kn-type dehydrin from Group I and one of the six TaDHNs analyzed by RT-qPCR under hormone treatments; Group I DHNs are particularly responsive to cold and drought.
Plant_Genes.Summary_key_Findings_of_Core_Gene:
1. TaDHN13-A encodes a K3-type Kn dehydrin and clusters with Group I DHNs (DHN8/9/10/13).
2. Group I DHNs are mainly up-regulated by cold and drought and sensitive to high salinity, but are frequently down-regulated during B. graminis and P. striiformis infection.
3. RT-qPCR shows that TaDHN13-A is strongly induced by ABA and MeJA, with MeJA-induced expression peaking at 6 h; SA and GA have weaker or no positive effects.
4. This pattern suggests that TaDHN13-A contributes to ABA- and MeJA-mediated abiotic stress responses in wheat.
Plant_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq datasets across tissues, abiotic stresses, and pathogen infections (SRP043554, SRP045409, SRP300360, SRP041017, ERP013829, ERP107574) were used to evaluate TaDHN13-A expression; RT-qPCR assessed ABA-, GA-, SA-, and MeJA-induced expression dynamics.
Plant_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Kn-type dehydrin that is part of the cold- and drought-inducible Group I DHNs and shows strong ABA- and MeJA-induced expression, indicating a role in hormone-regulated abiotic stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Regulatory_Pathway: ABA signaling (strong positive regulation of TaDHN13-A expression) and jasmonate (MeJA) signaling (induction peaking at 6 h), associated with cold, drought, and high-salinity stress responses.
Plant_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dehydrin (DHN) family protein; K3-type Kn dehydrin.
Plant_Genes.Expression_Pattern: TaDHN13-A, as a Group I Kn-type DHN, is mainly up-regulated under cold and drought and shows sensitivity to high salinity, while being mostly down-regulated during B. graminis and P. striiformis infections. RT-qPCR shows strong ABA-induced expression and MeJA-induced expression peaking at 6 h; SA and GA exert weak or non-inductive effects.
Plant_Genes.Reference_Genome_Version: IWGSC v1.0 bread wheat reference genome
Plant_Genes.Key_Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Cold, drought, heat, and salt stresses; pathogen infections (F. graminearum, B. graminis, P. striiformis); hormone treatments with ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA on Chinese Spring seedlings.
Plant_Genes.Research_Topic: Contribution of Kn-type dehydrin TaDHN13-A to ABA- and MeJA-mediated responses to cold, drought, and salt stress in wheat.
Plant_Genes.Trait_Category: Abiotic stress tolerance (cold, drought, high salinity) and hormone-responsive gene expression (ABA, MeJA).
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Materials: Chinese Spring seedlings treated with abiotic stresses and hormone solutions (ABA, GA, SA, MeJA) in a growth chamber; RNA isolated for RNA-seq and RT-qPCR analyses.
Plant_Genes.Experimental_Methods: In silico identification of TaDHN13-A as a K3-type Kn dehydrin; RNA-seq profiling under multiple stresses; RT-qPCR time-course under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA treatments to assess hormone-dependent expression.
Plant_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR time-course of TaDHN13-A expression under ABA, GA, SA, and MeJA treatments combined with RNA-seq stress-expression profiles to validate its hormone and abiotic-stress responsiveness.
Plant_Genes.Gene_Discovery_or_Cloning_Method: Identified through HMMER/BLASTP mining of the wheat genome using the dehydrin domain HMM PF00257, followed by phylogenetic assignment to Group I Kn-type DHNs and localization to chromosome 7A.
Plant_Genes.Breeding_Application_Value: TaDHN13-A, as a cold- and drought-inducible, strongly ABA- and MeJA-responsive Kn-type dehydrin, is a candidate for breeding or engineering wheat with enhanced tolerance to dehydration-related stresses and for dissecting hormone–stress crosstalk in dehydrin-mediated protection.
